settings_instance_subtitle: Here you can configure general instance parameters.
settings_instance_section_core_descripti: Configure the primary instance identity, URLs and session lifetime used across the platform.
settings_instance_section_data_retention: Control if VPN statistics are purged automatically and how long they are kept.
settings_instance_section_password_descr: Set the token expiration time when a password is changed in the system.
settings_instance_error_invalid_host: Defguard URL must use a hostname, not an IP address
settings_certs_ca_description: Manage certificates automatically generated by Defguard for secure communication. These certificates are used to authenticate devices and services, ensuring encrypted connections across your network.
settings_certs_ca_summary: The system created all required certificate files, including the root certificate and private key. You can download these files and continue with the configuration.
settings_certs_ca_certificate_validated: Your uploaded Certificate has been successfully validated. All required files were checked and confirmed as correct and ready for use. You can download the validated certificate files if needed for your setup.
settings_certs_certs_description: Add and manage certificates issued outside of Defguard. Use them to integrate existing infrastructure and maintain secure connections with external systems and services.
settings_certs_core_wizard_subtitle: Reconfigure how Defguard secures the internal Core URL.
settings_certs_core_wizard_step_internal: Choose how SSL should be configured for the internal Core URL.
Review the SSL configuration generated from your selected certificate type.
settings_certs_core_wizard_step_summary_: Review the result of the certificate configuration.
settings_certs_core_wizard_summary_succe: Your Core certificate settings have been saved successfully.
settings_certs_core_wizard_summary_url_n: If the Core URL scheme or host should change after this certificate update, review the Defguard URL in Instance settings.
settings_certs_certs_none_description: You have opted to deploy a custom Reverse Proxy and manage your own SSL certificates.
settings_certs_certs_internal_descriptio: Manage certificates automatically generated by Defguard for secure communication.
settings_certs_certs_custom_description: You are using your own, custom SSL certificate.
settings_certs_certs_letsencrypt_descrip: You are using a "Let's Encrypt"® issued certificate.
settings_certs_edge_wizard_subtitle: Reconfigure how Defguard secures the public Edge URL.
settings_certs_edge_wizard_step_external: Choose how SSL should be configured for the public Edge URL.
Review the SSL configuration generated from your selected certificate type.
settings_certs_edge_wizard_step_summary_: Review the result of the certificate configuration.
settings_certs_edge_wizard_summary_succe: Your Edge certificate settings have been saved successfully.
settings_certs_edge_wizard_summary_url_n: If the public Edge URL scheme or host should change after this certificate update, review the Public Edge Component URL in Instance settings.
settings_enrollment_page_subtitle: Configure enrollment settings to control how users join your instance and manage the enrollment flow.
settings_enrollment_message_templates_su: This message will be shown at the end of enrollment process. Enrollment is a process by which a new employee will be able to activate their new account, create a password and configure a VPN device.
settings_enrollment_template_display_mes: This information will be displayed for user in service once enrollment is completed. We advise to insert links and explain next steps briefly.
settings_enrollment_template_send_email_: This information will be sent to user email once enrollment is completed. We advise to insert links and explain next steps briefly.
settings_enrollment_template_same_as_mes: Your email will contain the same text as the welcome message from the input above.
settings_enrollment_template_help_admin_: first name of the administrator who initiated the enrollment process
last name of the administrator who initiated the enrollment process
phone number of the administrator who initiated the enrollment process
email of the administrator who initiated the enrollment process
settings_enrollment_template_help_defgua: internal Defguard URL (your Defguard instance address)
settings_enrollment_section_duration_des: Configure the expiration time of the unique token sent to a newly added user. This token is used to activate the account, and in this section administrators can control how long the token remains valid.
settings_general_section_client_behavior: Manage how users interact with the Defguard client. Control device management permissions, configuration access, and traffic routing options.
settings_activity_log_streaming_descript: Monitor and export real-time activity logs from your Defguard instance. Stream events to external systems for auditing, analytics, or security monitoring.
settings_activity_log_streaming_no_upstr: You don't have any activity log upstreams.
Click the button below to add an activity log provider and start streaming events.
settings_activity_log_streaming_delete_l: Failed to delete log stream destination.
settings_license_subtitle: Manage your Defguard license, view usage details, and track plan limits.
settings_license_key_description: Enter a license key to unlock additional Defguard features, your key will be sent via email after purchase or registration.
settings_license_choose_plan_title: Choose plan that matches your needs
settings_license_expand_plan_title: Expand your possibilities with advanced plans
settings_license_plan_business_descripti: External SSO & SIEM integration, LDAP/Active Directory, Firewall Management, REST API, Real-time clients configuration updates and more!
settings_license_plan_business_promotion: A free Business license is available on our pricing page.
settings_license_plan_enterprise_descrip: Expand your security with: High Availability, Pre-logon/Always-on VPN, and upcoming support for Device Posture and Hardware based MFA.
settings_license_expired_banner: Your license key has expired. Please renew your license to continue using Defguard {tier} features.
settings_license_expiring_soon_banner: Your license will expire in {days} days. Please renew your license to avoid losing access to the features included in your current plan.
settings_license_expired_notice_descript: Defguard will continue to work for {duration}, after which it will downgrade to the Open Source version. Please renew your license as soon as possible to avoid losing access to the features included in your current plan.
Your license has expired. Please renew your license to continue using Defguard {tier} features.
settings_notifications_smtp_card_content: Configure your SMTP server to enable email notifications and system alerts.
settings_smtp_reset_confirm_body: Are you sure you want to reset SMTP settings? This action cannot be undone.
settings_smtp_subtitle: Configure the SMTP server used to send system messages to users.
settings_smtp_section_server_description: Configure the SMTP server here — it’s required for sending system messages to users.
settings_smtp_test_email_description: Check if your SMTP configuration works by sending a test email.
settings_ldap_subtitle: Manage LDAP/Active Directory connection details, user and group mapping, and synchronization rules that determine how users are imported and updated in Defguard.
settings_ldap_section_connection_descrip: Configure LDAP connection settings here. These settings determine how Defguard connects to your LDAP server. Encrypted connections are also supported (StartTLS, LDAPS).
settings_ldap_section_user_description: Configure LDAP user settings here. These settings determine how Defguard maps and synchronizes LDAP user information with local users.
settings_ldap_section_group_description: Configure LDAP group settings here. These settings determine how Defguard maps and synchronizes LDAP group information with local groups.
settings_ldap_section_sync_description: Control how Defguard synchronizes users with LDAP — disable sync, import users from LDAP, or keep both systems updated automatically.
settings_ldap_section_sync_warning: To enable LDAP synchronization, please fill out the form above first.
settings_ldap_sync_one_way_content: Changes made to users in Defguard are propagated to your LDAP directory.
settings_ldap_sync_two_way_content: Defguard and LDAP stay in sync. Changes made in either system can be synchronized, and the selected authority decides which data wins if conflicts occur.
settings_ldap_authority_block_descriptio: Select whether user data should be controlled by LDAP or by Defguard. The selected system becomes the primary source and can overwrite the other one when differences appear.
settings_ldap_authority_defguard_title: Defguard is the source of truth
settings_ldap_authority_defguard_content: Defguard data overrides LDAP records. User and group information in LDAP will be updated to match Defguard during synchronization.
settings_ldap_authority_ldap_title: LDAP is the source of truth
settings_ldap_authority_ldap_content: LDAP directory data overrides Defguard records. User and group information in Defguard will be updated to match LDAP during synchronization.
settings_ldap_test_connection_tooltip: To test connection please fill the form and save the changes first.
settings_ldap_section_remote_enrollment_: Control how Defguard synchronizes users with LDAP — disable sync, import users from LDAP, or keep both systems updated automatically.
settings_ldap_helper_remote_enrollment_e: When Defguard is integrated with AD/LDAP, users can log in with their directory credentials and complete remote enrollment (including MFA) via the desktop client. When this switch is enabled, newly created users can be Enrolled.
settings_ldap_label_remote_enrollment_se: Automatically invite new AD/LDAP users to enroll
settings_ldap_helper_remote_enrollment_s: When users are synced via AD/LDAP, Defguard can automatically send an enrollment email for device setup and MFA. If disabled, enrollment must be started manually by an admin.
settings_ldap_remote_enrollment_warning_: Please activate LDAP synchronization and set up the SMTP server first to enable this feature.
Please configure the SMTP server to enable this feature.
settings_client_subtitle: Manage user permissions and configuration options for device control, WireGuard setup, and VPN routing.
settings_client_permissions_description: Define which VPN client settings users can modify and which are restricted.
settings_client_device_management_conten: When this option is on, only Admins can manage devices in user profiles.
settings_client_wireguard_configuration_: When this option is on, users can't view or download manual WireGuard configurations. Only Defguard desktop client setup will be available.
settings_client_traffic_policy_descripti: Specify the conditions that determine how traffic should behave in the application.
settings_client_traffic_policy_none_cont: When this option is enabled, users will be able to select all routing options.
settings_client_traffic_policy_disable_a: When this option is enabled, users will not be able to route all traffic through the VPN.
settings_client_traffic_policy_force_all: When this option is enabled, the users will always route all traffic through the VPN.
settings_gateway_notifications_subtitle: Here you can manage email notifications.
settings_notifications_gateway_card_cont: Configure admin email notifications for gateway disconnect and reconnect events, and set the inactivity threshold that triggers disconnect notifications.
settings_gateway_notifications_smtp_warn: To enable notifications you must first configure SMTP.
settings_gateway_notifications_disconnec: Send an email notification to admin users when a gateway disconnects.
settings_gateway_notifications_reconnect: Send an email notification to admin users when a gateway reconnects.
